recap: Jude Bellingham's brace lifted England past Norway 2-1 in extra time, with his dramatic equalizer in the first half coming under controversial circumstances after the ball appeared to clip an aerial camera cable before reaching him. Norway's Andreas Schjelderup had given the underdogs the lead in the 36th minute, and Erling Haaland was denied by goalkeeper Jordan Pickford and had a rebound goal disallowed by VAR, leaving the Manchester City star scoreless for the first time in the tournament. Bellingham struck again in the 123rd minute to send England to the semifinals and end Norway's fairytale run that had captured imaginations with their "Viking row" celebration and Haaland's seven-goal haul.
